articleTitle: Effectiveness of mHealth-Based Nutritional Interventions on Iron Status of Pregnant Women: Systematic Review of Randomized Controlled Trials.
takeaways: Mobile health apps and digital platforms show promise as effective tools for delivering nutritional interventions to pregnant women, particularly in areas with limited healthcare access

Iron deficiency anemia remains disproportionately high among pregnant women in low-resource regions, making targeted nutritional interventions through accessible technology platforms a critical public health priority
Poor adherence to iron supplementation and gaps in nutrition knowledge are major barriers that mHealth interventions can help address through improved education and counseling delivery
Digital health interventions offer a scalable solution to overcome traditional healthcare delivery limitations for pregnant women who struggle with accessing regular prenatal nutrition counseling
